lede: A look at May 11 through the almanac — historic events, famous birthdays including Abou Diaby and Salvador Dalí, and the day's notable deaths and observances.

events.title: Shireen Abu Akleh
events.desc: Palestinian-American journalist Shireen Abu Akleh is killed while covering a raid in Jenin. Israel eventually admitted and apologized for the murder, after initial denials.

events.title: 2011 Lorca earthquake
events.desc: An earthquake of magnitude 5.1 hits Lorca, Spain.

events.title: David Cameron
events.desc: David Cameron takes office as Prime Minister of the United Kingdom as the Conservatives and Liberal Democrats form the country's first coalition government since the Second World War.

events.title: Pokhran-II
events.desc: India conducts three underground atomic tests in Pokhran.

events.title: Deep Blue (chess computer)
events.desc: Deep Blue, a chess-playing supercomputer, defeats Garry Kasparov in the last game of the rematch, becoming the first computer to beat a world-champion chess player in a classic match format.

events.title: Wham Paymaster robbery
events.desc: An attack upon a U.S. Army paymaster and escort results in the theft of over $28,000 and the award of two Medals of Honor.

events.title: William Lawson (explorer)
events.desc: William Lawson, Gregory Blaxland and William Wentworth discover a route across the Blue Mountains, opening up inland Australia to settlement.

events.title: Spencer Perceval
events.desc: Prime Minister Spencer Perceval is assassinated by John Bellingham in the lobby of the British House of Commons.

events.title: Diamond Sutra
events.desc: A copy of the Diamond Sūtra is published, the earliest dated and printed book known.

births.name: Richard Feynman
births.line: American physicist and engineer, Nobel Prize laureate

deaths.text: Karl Schwarzschild, German astronomer and physicist : xix
Spencer Perceval, English lawyer and politician, Prime Minister of the United Kingdom
Leo VI the Wise, Byzantine Emperor, the second ruler of the Macedonian dynasty

observances.text: Christian feast day: Mamertus, the first of the Ice Saints
